---
title: "Reptalytics: NYC Restaurant Inspection Analysis"
subtitle: "Exploring Patterns, Violations, and Insights Across New York City's Dining Landscape"
author:
  - name: "Randi Shimsky-Agosto"
  - name: "Haley Vargas"
  - name: "Victor Murra Schott"
  - name: "Raúl J. Solá Navarro"
date: last-modified
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: docs/custom.css
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    smooth-scroll: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    code-tools: true
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
    highlight-style: github
    html-math-method: katex
    include-before-body: docs/title_banner.html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  cache: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import scipy.stats as stats
import requests
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

pd.set_option("display.max_columns", 20)
pd.set_option("display.float_format", "{:.2f}".format)

import plotly.io as pio
pio.templates.default = "plotly_white"

NAVY    = "#1B3A6B"
STEEL   = "#2E6DA4"
CORAL   = "#C94F3D"
GOLD    = "#E09B1A"
MINT    = "#2A9490"
PALETTE = [NAVY, STEEL, CORAL, GOLD, MINT, "#7B4E9E", "#4CAF50"]

# ── Helper: styled pandas table ──────────────────────────────────────────────
def styled_table(df, hide_index=True):
    s = df.style.set_table_attributes('class="reptalytics-table"')
    if hide_index:
        s = s.hide(axis="index")
    return s
```

```{python}
#| label: load-data
#| include: false

APP_TOKEN = None
BASE_URL  = "https://data.cityofnewyork.us/resource/43nn-pn8j.json"
LIMIT     = 50_000
COLS      = (
    "camis,dba,boro,cuisine_description,inspection_date,"
    "action,violation_code,violation_description,"
    "critical_flag,score,grade"
)

def fetch_all(base_url, limit=50_000, cols=None):
    headers = {"X-App-Token": APP_TOKEN} if APP_TOKEN else {}
    frames, offset = [], 0
    while True:
        params = {"$limit": limit, "$offset": offset, "$order": ":id"}
        if cols:
            params["$select"] = cols
        resp = requests.get(base_url, params=params, headers=headers, timeout=120)
        resp.raise_for_status()
        batch = resp.json()
        if not batch:
            break
        frames.append(pd.DataFrame(batch))
        offset += limit
        if len(batch) < limit:
            break
    return pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)

raw = fetch_all(BASE_URL, limit=LIMIT, cols=COLS)
```

```{python}
#| label: clean-data
#| include: false

raw = raw.rename(columns={
    "camis": "Restaurant ID", "dba": "Restaurant Name",
    "boro": "Borough", "cuisine_description": "Cuisine Description",
    "inspection_date": "Inspection Date", "action": "Action",
    "violation_code": "Violation Code", "violation_description": "Violation Description",
    "critical_flag": "Critical Flag", "score": "Score", "grade": "Grade",
})

raw["Inspection Date"] = pd.to_datetime(raw["Inspection Date"], errors="coerce")
raw["Score"]           = pd.to_numeric(raw["Score"], errors="coerce")
raw["Year"]            = raw["Inspection Date"].dt.year
raw["Month"]           = raw["Inspection Date"].dt.month

df = raw[raw["Year"].between(2021, 2024)].copy()
df = df[~df["Borough"].isin(["0", "Missing", "", None])]
df = df.dropna(subset=["Borough"])

unique_inspections = df.drop_duplicates(subset=["Restaurant ID", "Inspection Date"]).copy()
```

---

## Introduction

New York City operates one of the most rigorous restaurant inspection programs in the world. Every restaurant is inspected at least once a year by the NYC Department of Health and Mental Hygiene (DOHMH), receiving a numerical **violation score** (lower = better) and a letter **grade** posted publicly on the front door.

This report analyzes publicly available inspection data from **2021 through 2024** to answer five questions:

1. **Trend**: How have the number of inspections and average violation scores changed over time?
2. **Borough**: Do inspection grades differ significantly across the five boroughs?
3. **Closures**: Which boroughs see the highest restaurant closure rates, and is that rate rising?
4. **Cuisine**: Which cuisine categories accumulate the highest average violation scores?
5. **Violations**: What are the most common violation codes, and do they follow seasonal patterns?

::: {.callout-note}
Data is pulled live from the [NYC Open Data SODA API](https://data.cityofnewyork.us/resource/43nn-pn8j.json) each time this document renders.
:::

---

## Exploratory Data Analysis

```{python}
#| label: eda-stats
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

from IPython.display import display, HTML

rows_fetched = len(raw)
rows_filtered = len(df)
unique_insp = len(unique_inspections)
boroughs = sorted(df["Borough"].dropna().unique())
n_cols = df.shape[1]

stats_html = f"""
<div class="stat-grid">
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-value">{rows_fetched:,}</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Total Rows Fetched</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-value">{rows_filtered:,}</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Rows (2021–2024)</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-value">{unique_insp:,}</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Unique Inspections</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-value">{n_cols}</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Columns</div>
  </div>
</div>
"""
display(HTML(stats_html))
```

```{python}
#| label: eda-schema
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

schema = pd.DataFrame({
    "Column": df.dtypes.index,
    "Type":   df.dtypes.astype(str).values,
    "Non-Null": df.notnull().sum().values,
    "% Missing": (df.isnull().mean() * 100).round(1).values,
})
styled_table(schema)
```

```{python}
#| label: eda-sample
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

sample_cols = ["Restaurant Name", "Borough", "Cuisine Description",
               "Inspection Date", "Violation Code", "Score", "Grade"]
styled_table(
    df[sample_cols].sample(8, random_state=42).reset_index(drop=True)
)
```

```{python}
#| label: eda-nulls
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

null_pct = (df.isnull().sum() / len(df) * 100).round(1).reset_index()
null_pct.columns = ["Column", "% Missing"]

fig = px.bar(
    null_pct.sort_values("% Missing", ascending=False),
    x="Column", y="% Missing",
    title="Missing Values by Column (%)",
    color="% Missing",
    color_continuous_scale=["#2A9490", "#E09B1A", "#C94F3D"],
    text_auto=".1f",
)
fig.update_layout(xaxis_tickangle=-30, showlegend=False, height=380)
fig.show()
```

---

## Q1: Inspection Volume & Violation Score Trends {#sec-q1}

> **Question:** How have the total number of inspections and average violation scores changed from 2021 to 2024?

```{python}
#| label: q1-compute
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

yearly_inspections = (
    unique_inspections.groupby("Year").size().reset_index(name="Total Inspections")
)
yearly_avg_score = (
    unique_inspections.dropna(subset=["Score"])
    .groupby("Year")["Score"].mean()
    .reset_index(name="Avg Violation Score")
)
yearly_summary = yearly_inspections.merge(yearly_avg_score, on="Year")
yearly_summary["Avg Violation Score"] = yearly_summary["Avg Violation Score"].round(2)

styled_table(yearly_summary)
```

```{python}
#| label: q1-plot-inspections
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

fig1 = px.line(
    yearly_summary, x="Year", y="Total Inspections",
    markers=True,
    title="Total Inspections by Year",
    labels={"Total Inspections": "Number of Inspections"},
    color_discrete_sequence=[STEEL],
)
fig1.update_traces(line_width=3, marker_size=10)
fig1.update_layout(
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=[2021, 2022, 2023, 2024]),
    height=380,
)
fig1.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: q1-plot-scores
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

fig2 = px.line(
    yearly_summary, x="Year", y="Avg Violation Score",
    markers=True,
    title="Average Violation Score by Year  <span style='font-size:0.8em;color:#888'>(lower = better)</span>",
    labels={"Avg Violation Score": "Avg Violation Score"},
    color_discrete_sequence=[CORAL],
)
fig2.update_traces(line_width=3, marker_size=10)
fig2.update_layout(
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=[2021, 2022, 2023, 2024]),
    height=380,
)
fig2.show()
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**Key finding:** Both the number of inspections *and* average violation scores have risen steadily since 2021. The inspection activity rebounded after COVID-era slowdowns, and the rising average score suggests compliance challenges citywide.
:::

---

## Q2: Grade Differences Across Boroughs {#sec-q2}

> **Question:** Do inspection grades differ significantly across NYC's five boroughs? (ANOVA test)

```{python}
#| label: q2-compute
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

grade_df = df[["Restaurant ID", "Borough", "Inspection Date", "Grade"]].copy()
grade_df = grade_df[~grade_df["Grade"].isin(["P", "N", "Z", "Not Yet Graded"])]
grade_df = grade_df.dropna(subset=["Grade"])
grade_unique = grade_df.drop_duplicates(subset=["Restaurant ID", "Inspection Date"]).copy()

grade_map = {"A": 3, "B": 2, "C": 1}
grade_unique["Grade_Num"] = grade_unique["Grade"].map(grade_map)
grade_unique["Year"]      = grade_unique["Inspection Date"].dt.year

avg_grade_borough = (
    grade_unique.groupby("Borough")["Grade_Num"]
    .mean().sort_values(ascending=False).reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"Grade_Num": "Avg Grade Score"})
)
avg_grade_borough["Avg Grade Score"] = avg_grade_borough["Avg Grade Score"].round(4)

styled_table(avg_grade_borough)
```

```{python}
#| label: q2-plot-bar
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

fig = px.bar(
    avg_grade_borough, x="Borough", y="Avg Grade Score",
    color="Borough", color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text=avg_grade_borough["Avg Grade Score"].round(2),
    title="Average Inspection Grade by Borough  <span style='font-size:0.8em;color:#888'>(A=3, B=2, C=1)</span>",
)
fig.update_layout(yaxis_range=[2.5, 3.05], showlegend=False, height=400)
fig.update_traces(textposition="outside")
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: q2-plot-yoy
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

avg_yoy = (
    grade_unique.groupby(["Year", "Borough"])["Grade_Num"]
    .mean().reset_index().rename(columns={"Grade_Num": "Avg Grade Score"})
)

fig2 = px.line(
    avg_yoy, x="Year", y="Avg Grade Score", color="Borough",
    markers=True, color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    title="Average Inspection Grade by Borough — Year Over Year",
    labels={"Avg Grade Score": "Avg Grade Score (A=3, B=2, C=1)"},
)
fig2.update_layout(
    yaxis_range=[2.5, 3.05],
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=[2021, 2022, 2023, 2024]),
    height=420,
)
fig2.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: q2-anova
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

from IPython.display import display, HTML

borough_groups = [
    grade_unique[grade_unique["Borough"] == b]["Grade_Num"].dropna()
    for b in grade_unique["Borough"].unique()
]
f_stat, p_value = stats.f_oneway(*borough_groups)
sig = p_value < 0.05

anova_html = f"""
<div class="anova-box">
  <div class="anova-title">One-Way ANOVA Results</div>
  <div class="anova-grid">
    <div class="anova-item">
      <span class="anova-label">F-statistic</span>
      <span class="anova-value">{f_stat:.4f}</span>
    </div>
    <div class="anova-item">
      <span class="anova-label">P-value</span>
      <span class="anova-value">{p_value:.2e}</span>
    </div>
    <div class="anova-item anova-result {'anova-sig' if sig else 'anova-ns'}">
      <span>{"✓ Statistically significant (p < 0.05)" if sig else "✗ Not statistically significant"}</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
"""
display(HTML(anova_html))
```

::: {.callout-note}
**ANOVA result:** With an F-statistic above 15 and a p-value well below 0.05, we reject the null hypothesis. Borough membership is a statistically significant predictor of inspection grade as we see that Staten Island and Manhattan consistently outperform the Bronx and Queens.
:::

---

## Q3: Restaurant Closure Rates {#sec-q3}

> **Question:** How do restaurant closure rates compare across boroughs, and how has the rate changed over time?

```{python}
#| label: q3-compute
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

closure_df = df.copy()
closure_df["Year"] = closure_df["Inspection Date"].dt.year
closure_df = closure_df.dropna(subset=["Year"])
closure_df["Year"] = closure_df["Year"].astype(int)
closure_df["Inspection ID"] = (
    closure_df["Restaurant ID"].astype(str) + "_" +
    closure_df["Inspection Date"].astype(str)
)

closures_mask = closure_df["Action"].str.contains(
    "Establishment Closed by DOHMH", case=False, na=False
)

insp_boro = closure_df.groupby("Borough")["Inspection ID"].nunique().reset_index(name="Total Inspections")
closures_boro = closure_df[closures_mask].groupby("Borough")["Inspection ID"].nunique().reset_index(name="Total Closures")
merged_boro = insp_boro.merge(closures_boro, on="Borough", how="left")
merged_boro["Total Closures"] = merged_boro["Total Closures"].fillna(0).astype(int)
merged_boro["Closure Rate (%)"] = (merged_boro["Total Closures"] / merged_boro["Total Inspections"] * 100).round(2)
merged_boro = merged_boro.sort_values("Closure Rate (%)", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

insp_year = closure_df.groupby("Year")["Inspection ID"].nunique().reset_index(name="Total Inspections")
closures_year = closure_df[closures_mask].groupby("Year")["Inspection ID"].nunique().reset_index(name="Total Closures")
merged_year = insp_year.merge(closures_year, on="Year", how="left")
merged_year["Total Closures"] = merged_year["Total Closures"].fillna(0).astype(int)
merged_year["Closure Rate (%)"] = (merged_year["Total Closures"] / merged_year["Total Inspections"] * 100).round(2)

styled_table(merged_boro)
```

```{python}
#| label: q3-plot-year
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

fig_yr = px.line(
    merged_year, x="Year", y="Closure Rate (%)",
    markers=True,
    title="Restaurant Closure Rate by Year (%)",
    color_discrete_sequence=[STEEL],
)
fig_yr.update_traces(line_width=3, marker_size=10)
fig_yr.update_layout(
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=[2021, 2022, 2023, 2024]),
    height=380,
)
fig_yr.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: q3-plot-borough
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

fig_bo = px.bar(
    merged_boro, x="Borough", y="Closure Rate (%)",
    color="Borough", color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text=merged_boro["Closure Rate (%)"].apply(lambda v: f"{v:.2f}%"),
    title="Restaurant Closure Rate by Borough (%)",
)
fig_bo.update_traces(textposition="outside")
fig_bo.update_layout(showlegend=False, height=400)
fig_bo.show()
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**Key finding:** The citywide closure rate has risen year-over-year since 2021. At the borough level, Brooklyn has the highest closure rate while Staten Island is substantially lower as demonstrated consistently with its better average grades.
:::

---

## Q4: Average Violation Score by Cuisine Category {#sec-q4}

> **Question:** Which broad cuisine categories receive the highest average violation scores?

```{python}
#| label: q4-categorize
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

CATEGORIES = {
    "Asian":           ["Pakistani","Indian","Bangladeshi","Japanese","Chinese","Filipino",
                        "Thai","Korean","Southeast Asian","Asian/Asian Fusion",
                        "Chinese/Japanese","Indonesian","Afghan"],
    "European":        ["French","New French","Irish","German","Polish","Russian",
                        "Scandinavian","English","Continental","Czech","Basque",
                        "Armenian","Eastern European","Portuguese","Spanish","Greek",
                        "Turkish","Italian"],
    "American":        ["American","New American","Tex-Mex","Southwestern","Soul Food",
                        "Steakhouse","Californian","Hawaiian","Barbecue","Hamburgers",
                        "Hotdogs","Hotdogs/Pretzels","Pizza","Donuts",
                        "Pancakes/Waffles","Seafood","Chicken"],
    "Latin American":  ["Peruvian","Brazilian","Chilean","Mexican","Latin American",
                        "Caribbean","Tapas","Chimichurri","Creole","Creole/Cajun",
                        "Cajun","Chinese/Cuban"],
    "Beverages/Desserts": ["Coffee/Tea","Bakery Products/Desserts","Frozen Desserts",
                           "Juice, Smoothies, Fruit Salads","Bottled Beverages",
                           "Nuts/Confectionary"],
    "Mixed/Other":     ["Jewish/Kosher","Moroccan","Egyptian","Lebanese","Iranian",
                        "Middle Eastern","African","Ethiopian","Australian","Polynesian",
                        "Vegetarian","Vegan","Fruits/Vegetables","Fusion","Mediterranean",
                        "Other","Not Listed/Not Applicable","Soups/Salads/Sandwiches",
                        "Sandwiches","Sandwiches/Salads/Mixed Buffet","Bagels/Pretzels",
                        "Salads","Soups","Haute Cuisine"],
}

def categorize_cuisine(cuisine):
    clow = str(cuisine).lower().strip()
    for cat, kws in CATEGORIES.items():
        for kw in kws:
            if kw.lower() in clow:
                return cat
    return "Mixed/Other"

cuisine_df = df.dropna(subset=["Score", "Cuisine Description"]).copy()
cuisine_df["Cuisine Category"] = cuisine_df["Cuisine Description"].apply(categorize_cuisine)
cuisine_unique = cuisine_df.drop_duplicates(subset=["Restaurant ID", "Inspection Date"]).copy()

avg_score_cuisine = (
    cuisine_unique.groupby("Cuisine Category")["Score"]
    .mean().reset_index(name="Avg Violation Score")
    .sort_values("Avg Violation Score", ascending=False)
)
avg_score_cuisine["Avg Violation Score"] = avg_score_cuisine["Avg Violation Score"].round(2)

styled_table(avg_score_cuisine.reset_index(drop=True))
```

```{python}
#| label: q4-plot
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

fig = px.bar(
    avg_score_cuisine, x="Cuisine Category", y="Avg Violation Score",
    color="Cuisine Category", color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    text="Avg Violation Score",
    title="Average Inspection Violation Score by Cuisine Category  <span style='font-size:0.8em;color:#888'>(lower = better)</span>",
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False,
    yaxis_range=[0, avg_score_cuisine["Avg Violation Score"].max() * 1.15],
    height=450)
fig.update_traces(textposition="outside", texttemplate="%{text:.1f}")
fig.show()
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Key finding:** Asian cuisine restaurants consistently receive the highest average violation scores, while Beverages/Desserts establishments score lowest. This may reflect inspection complexity in kitchens handling raw proteins, live seafood, and complex preparation techniques face more scrutiny.
:::

---

## Q5: Most Common Violations & Seasonal Patterns {#sec-q5}

> **Question:** What are the top 5 violation codes, and do violations follow seasonal patterns?

```{python}
#| label: q5-compute
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

viol_df = df.dropna(subset=["Violation Code"]).copy()
viol_df = viol_df[viol_df["Violation Code"].str.strip() != ""]

code_to_desc = (
    viol_df.groupby("Violation Code")["Violation Description"]
    .agg(lambda x: x.value_counts().idxmax())
)
viol_df["Violation Description"] = viol_df["Violation Code"].map(code_to_desc)

code_crit = (
    viol_df.groupby("Violation Code")["Critical Flag"]
    .agg(lambda x: x.value_counts().idxmax())
    .str.lower().str.strip()
)

top5_codes = viol_df["Violation Code"].value_counts().head(5).index.tolist()
top5_df = viol_df[viol_df["Violation Code"].isin(top5_codes)].copy()

summary_table = (
    top5_df.groupby(["Violation Code", "Violation Description"])
    .size().reset_index(name="Total Count")
    .sort_values("Total Count", ascending=False)
)
summary_table["Severity"] = summary_table["Violation Code"].map(
    lambda c: "🔴 Critical" if code_crit.get(c, "") in ["critical", "y", "yes"] else "🔵 Non-Critical"
)
summary_table["Violation Description"] = summary_table["Violation Description"].str[:60] + "…"

styled_table(summary_table.reset_index(drop=True))
```

```{python}
#| label: q5-plot-bar
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

colors_bar = [
    CORAL if code_crit.get(c, "") in ["critical", "y", "yes"] else STEEL
    for c in top5_codes
]

fig = go.Figure()
for code, color in zip(top5_codes, colors_bar):
    row_data = summary_table[summary_table["Violation Code"] == code]
    count = int(row_data["Total Count"].values[0]) if len(row_data) else 0
    label = row_data["Severity"].values[0] if len(row_data) else ""
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=[code], y=[count], name=f"{code} ({label})",
        marker_color=color, text=[f"{count:,}"], textposition="outside",
        hovertemplate=f"<b>{code}</b><br>Count: {count:,}<br>{label}<extra></extra>",
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    title="Top 5 Most Common Violation Codes (2021–2024)<br>"
          "<sup>🔴 = Critical  |  🔵 = Non-Critical</sup>",
    xaxis_title="Violation Code", yaxis_title="Total Count",
    showlegend=True, height=460,
)
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: q5-plot-yoy
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

yearly_viol = (
    top5_df.groupby(["Year", "Violation Code"]).size().reset_index(name="Count")
)

fig2 = px.line(
    yearly_viol, x="Year", y="Count", color="Violation Code",
    markers=True, color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    title="Top 5 Violations — Year Over Year Trend",
    labels={"Count": "Violation Count"},
)
fig2.update_layout(xaxis=dict(tickvals=[2021, 2022, 2023, 2024]), height=420)
fig2.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: q5-seasonal
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

def month_to_season(m):
    if m in [12, 1, 2]: return "Winter"
    if m in [3, 4, 5]:  return "Spring"
    if m in [6, 7, 8]:  return "Summer"
    return "Autumn"

top5_df["Season"] = top5_df["Month"].apply(month_to_season)
seasonal = top5_df.groupby(["Season", "Violation Code"]).size().reset_index(name="Count")
season_order = ["Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Autumn"]
seasonal["Season"] = pd.Categorical(seasonal["Season"], categories=season_order, ordered=True)
seasonal = seasonal.sort_values("Season")

fig3 = px.bar(
    seasonal, x="Season", y="Count", color="Violation Code",
    barmode="group", color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    title="Top 5 Violations by Season",
    category_orders={"Season": season_order},
)
fig3.update_layout(height=420)
fig3.show()
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Key findings:**

- **10F** (non-food contact surfaces), although non-critical, is the single most common violation by a wide margin and a persistent but recurring issue.
- **08A** (rodent/pest susceptibility) and **06D** / **02G** (food safety) represent significant critical risks.
- Year-over-year trends show all top violations rising, consistent with increasing inspection volume.
- **No strong seasonal pattern** was detected. The violations are distributed relatively evenly across seasons, suggesting these are structural compliance issues rather than temperature- or season-driven problems.
:::

---

## Summary & Conclusions

```{python}
#| label: summary-table
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code"

summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "Question": [
        "Q1: Inspection & Score Trends",
        "Q2: Borough Grade Differences",
        "Q3: Closure Rates",
        "Q4: Cuisine & Violation Scores",
        "Q5: Most Common Violations",
    ],
    "Key Finding": [
        "Both inspection volume and average violation scores rose steadily 2021–2024.",
        "Grades differ significantly across boroughs (ANOVA p < 0.001); Staten Island leads.",
        "Citywide closure rate is rising; Brooklyn has the highest rate.",
        "Asian cuisine has the highest avg violation score (≈20); Beverages/Desserts lowest.",
        "10F (surface sanitation) dominates; no significant seasonal pattern detected.",
    ],
})
styled_table(summary)
```

### Recommendations

**For restaurant owners & operators:**

- Prioritize non-food contact surface cleaning (10F) as it is by far the most cited violation.
- Invest in pest-prevention infrastructure before inspection cycles, not after.
- Understand that as inspection frequency increases citywide, any lapse in routine sanitation is more likely to be caught.

**For consumers:**

- Always check the letter grade posted at the entrance before dining.
- Remember that grades fluctuate year-to-year (a current A does not guarantee perfection).

**For future analysis:**

- Cross-reference violation data with Yelp/Google ratings to explore whether diners notice or respond to grades.
- Investigate zip-code-level variation within boroughs.
- Incorporate census income data to test whether socioeconomic factors correlate with inspection outcomes.

---

## Data & Methods

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Data source** | NYC Open Data - DOHMH Restaurant Inspection Results |
| **API endpoint** | `https://data.cityofnewyork.us/resource/43nn-pn8j.json` |
| **Date range** | January 2021 - December 2024 |
| **Statistical test** | One-Way ANOVA (Q2) |
| **Visualization** | Plotly (interactive) |
| **Language** | Python 3 via Quarto + reticulate |
| **Rendered** | `quarto render reptalytics_report.qmd` |

---

*Reptalytics · Baruch College, Zicklin School of Business · CIS 9650 Python Programming for Analytics*

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## AI Usage Disclosure

This report was produced with assistance from AI tools. Specifically, AI was used to:

- Structure and author the Quarto document and its HTML/CSS styling
- Clean up and refactor Python code from the original Jupyter notebooks
- Create and improve several data visualizations
- General editing of text and narrative throughout the report

All analysis, research questions, findings, and interpretations are the original work of the Reptalytics team. The underlying data comes directly from NYC Open Data.
:::
